title: rosa rubiginosa var. densa
intro: Rosa rubiginosa var. densa, also known as the Scotch rose or dwarf sweet briar, is a compact, flowering shrub native to the British Isles, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its leaves, flowers, and stems, which contain anti-inflammatory compounds and flavonoids. Rosa rubiginosa var. densa has been used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be used in creams and salves for topical application.
